name: Biden v. Nebraska
summary: The Court found that Missouri had standing to challenge a federal student loan forgiveness program because MOHELA, though structured as a separate corporation, was sufficiently part of Missouri based on the state's control over it.
significance: NJ Transit argued this case supports a functional analysis that looks past corporate labels, as the Court found MOHELA was part of Missouri based on control and supervision factors. Plaintiffs distinguished it as a standing case with a different analytical framework.
